title: Work2Cash Documentation Portal
description: Find the exact product flow, build day, technical record or assurance control required for the current Work2Cash question.

readerRoutes.label: Understand product behavior
readerRoutes.summary: Read one standalone flow in plain language, including what starts it, what it depends on, what can go wrong and what happens next.
steps: Use its dependency and next-flow explanations without leaving the page.

readerRoutes.label: Build today’s assignment
readerRoutes.summary: Choose your team, active week and assigned day. Each day contains prerequisites, build order, tests, result and commit message.
steps: Open the active week and assigned day.
Download the one matching team-week agent pack when an AI agent is helping.

readerRoutes.label: Implement or review the system
readerRoutes.summary: Search contracts, data, platform operations or screens and link directly to one technical record.
steps: Treat provisional gaps as blockers rather than permission to invent.

readerRoutes.label: Test, approve or govern
readerRoutes.audience: QA, Legal, Privacy, Product and Operations
readerRoutes.summary: Open the exact QA suite, legal clause, release gate, decision, question or risk required for the review.
steps: Choose QA and Release, Legal and Compliance, or Decisions and Governance.
Change its state only when the named evidence and authorization exist.

readerRoutes.label: Prepare focused AI context
readerRoutes.summary: Start with AGENTS.md, then provide only the active team-week, assigned day and relevant flow or technical pack.
steps: Check authority in the Document Registry.
Use the smallest focused context set for implementation.
Use Full Project Agent Context only for onboarding or cross-project review.

libraries.label: Document Registry
libraries.description: Ownership, lifecycle, approval, authority and canonical/generated classification.

libraries.label: Standalone Flow Library
libraries.description: Seventy-two approved Mobile and Admin flows with local dependency and next-step explanations.

libraries.label: Junior Developer Plans
libraries.description: Team plans organized by week and day, with one agent pack for each team-week.

libraries.label: Contract Decision Library
libraries.description: Accepted contract decisions that close named implementation gaps.

libraries.label: Technical Reference Library
libraries.description: Contracts, data, platform operations and screens in four searchable families.

libraries.label: Assurance and Governance
libraries.description: QA/release, legal/compliance and decision/governance records in three focused registers.
libraries.audience: QA, Legal, Privacy, Product and Operations

agentDownloads.label: Repository agent instructions
agentDownloads.description: Authority, context selection and implementation guardrails.

agentDownloads.label: Full Project Agent Context
agentDownloads.description: Optional generated cross-project inventory for onboarding and consistency review.

agentDownloads.label: Agent Document Registry
agentDownloads.description: Machine-oriented ownership, lifecycle and authority view.

glossary.definition: A complete user or Admin journey with a clear beginning, outcome and next destination.
Reusable behavior used inside one or more main flows, such as OTP, media upload or permission recovery.
The agreed request, response, event or error shape that connected systems must follow.
A machine-readable description of the current HTTP API baseline; provisional gaps still block invention.
A business record, its fields, ownership, relationships, privacy rules and persistence readiness.
An external service such as payments, maps, identity verification, messaging or monitoring.
The approved delivery order for a team, separated into weeks and daily working outcomes.
One team-specific Markdown file containing Day 1 through Day 5 and the agent prompt for each day.
A required evidence checkpoint that must pass before an authorized Go/No-Go decision.
The file that must be edited because it controls its generated outputs.
A reproducible reading or agent file that inherits authority from its canonical source and must not be edited directly.
Usable for review and planning but not permission to invent unresolved behavior, schema or policy.
A test result, reviewed artifact, trace, screenshot, log or approval that proves a requirement or gate.
The approved source or qualified role that controls a decision when documents differ.
